context: Tests whether the agent follows UIKit Auto Layout and animation best practices when implementing an expandable card UI. The scenario covers constraint setup, batch activation, constraint reuse, animation patterns, lifecycle usage, and constraint identifiers.

checklist.name: translatesAutoresizingMask disabled
checklist.description: All programmatically created UIViews have translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints set to false before constraints are added

checklist.name: Batch constraint activation
checklist.description: Uses NSLayoutConstraint.activate([...]) to activate multiple constraints together — does NOT use individual .isActive = true calls for setting up constraints

checklist.name: Constraints created once
checklist.description: Height or visibility constraints for cards are created once (in init or viewDidLoad) and stored as properties — does NOT remove and recreate constraints on each tap

checklist.name: Toggle via isActive or constant
checklist.description: Expand/collapse is implemented by toggling constraint.isActive or modifying constraint.constant — not by creating new constraints on each state change

checklist.name: Constraint animation: flush first
checklist.description: Before animating constraint changes, calls layoutIfNeeded() on the superview to flush pending layout changes prior to starting the animation

checklist.name: Constraint animation: animate inside block
checklist.description: Calls layoutIfNeeded() inside the UIView.animate animation block (on superview) to drive the animated layout transition

checklist.name: No required priority changes at runtime
checklist.description: Any constraints whose priority is changed at runtime are initialized with a priority of 999 (or lower) — never created at .required (1000) and then changed

checklist.name: Constraint identifiers on key constraints
checklist.description: At least one key constraint (e.g. height or expanded constraint) has a constraint.identifier string set on it

checklist.name: viewDidLoad for one-time setup
checklist.description: Subview creation and constraint setup occurs in viewDidLoad — geometry-dependent work is NOT performed in viewDidLoad

checklist.name: viewDidLayoutSubviews correct usage
checklist.description: If viewDidLayoutSubviews is overridden, it is only used for bounds-dependent layer adjustments (e.g. cornerRadius) — NOT for one-time subview or constraint setup

checklist.name: super called in lifecycle overrides
checklist.description: Every overridden UIViewController lifecycle method (viewDidLoad, viewWillAppear, viewDidLayoutSubviews, etc.) calls super

checklist.name: UIStackView nesting depth
checklist.description: UIStackView nesting is at most 1–2 levels deep in any reusable cell or card view — does NOT have deeply nested stacks (3+ levels)

checklist.name: iOS 26+ flushUpdates availability gating
checklist.description: If the .flushUpdates animation option is used, it is wrapped in #available(iOS 26, *) with a fallback for older OS versions
